verdict: Electricians sit near the bottom of the AI risk distribution: physical work in unstructured environments, licensure, and life-safety stakes all push the score down. Demand is rising as electrification and data-center buildouts accelerate.
timeline_callout: AI helps with permit paperwork and load calculations now; the actual on-site work is durable for a generation.

routine_cognitive.reasoning: Code lookup and load calculations are routine cognitive — but a small share of the day.

creative_judgment.reasoning: Diagnosing intermittent faults in old buildings requires real judgment.

regulatory_moat.reasoning: Journeyman / master electrician licensure required for most work; permits inspected by code officials.

embodied_work.reasoning: Almost the entire role is on-site physical work in varied environments.

stakes_of_error.reasoning: Wiring errors cause fires and electrocutions; humans stay in the loop legally and ethically.

relationships.reasoning: Customer trust and contractor relationships matter for repeat work.

ai_capability_gap.reasoning: AI helps with code lookup and quoting; cannot pull cable through a wall.

physical_dexterity.reasoning: Fine motor skills in awkward spaces — robotics is far behind.

key_tasks.task: Install, maintain, and repair electrical wiring
Diagnose malfunctioning systems using test instruments
key_tasks.note: Requires physical inspection and contextual judgment.

key_tasks.task: Read blueprints and electrical code documents
key_tasks.note: AI assists with code lookup; the install does not move.

key_tasks.task: Pull permits and coordinate with inspectors
key_tasks.note: Paperwork is partly automatable; relationships and signoff are not.
AI accelerates estimation; trust closes the deal.

evidence_ledger.meta: US Dept. of Labor · Updated Jan 2026
evidence_ledger.contribution: Task list is overwhelmingly physical install + diagnostic.
Electrician exposure score is among the lowest — anchor for skilled trades.
Projects 11% growth — well above average; electrification + data-center buildout drive demand.
Skilled trades 14% automatable — automation hits paperwork, not the wires.
Renewable energy installers and electricians among top growing roles.

reasoning_steps.title: Score the eight dimensions
reasoning_steps.body: routine_cognitive=3, ai_capability_gap=2, creative_judgment=4, regulatory_moat=8, embodied_work=9, stakes_of_error=8, relationships=5, physical_dexterity=8.

reasoning_steps.title: Calibration check
reasoning_steps.body: Anchor table lists Electrician at 16. Computed 27 is 11 above the anchor. The anchor is set against Felten exposure data showing trades are even more resilient than the dimension math suggests; trust the anchor and soften toward 16.

adjacent_careers.name: Solar / EV Charging Installer
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Same skill set, fastest-growing electrification niche.

adjacent_careers.name: HVAC Technician
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Adjacent trade with the same moat structure.

adjacent_careers.name: Industrial Maintenance Tech
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Higher pay ceiling; works on factory floors with rising automation but humans still maintain the robots.

adjacent_careers.name: Electrical Contractor (Owner)
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Adds business + relationship work; you direct AI estimating tools instead of competing with them.

action_plan.title: Look into a registered apprenticeship (IBEW or local non-union)
action_plan.description: You earn while you learn; the licensure is the moat that makes this score low.

action_plan.title: Get OSHA 10 + a basic electrical safety cert this summer
action_plan.description: Cheap, fast, and a real signal on apprenticeship applications.

action_plan.title: Take physics and math seriously even though it's a trade
action_plan.description: Modern systems (solar, EV, smart panels) reward electricians who understand the engineering.

action_plan.title: Shadow an electrician for a week
action_plan.description: The work is physically demanding; confirm fit before committing to a 4-year apprenticeship.

follow_up_suggestions.label: What does an apprenticeship actually pay?
